mtrl.desc: qCFW - Arrives to Slim (2500-3000) & SuperSlim (4000-NOR) PlayStation 3 Models

br.div: UPDATE (Feb. 20) -PS3HEN 3.4.2 Open Beta: Test #1 Released- (Forum Link)UPDATE (Feb. 20) -qCFW updated(forum link) --qcfw-20260220-release(github link)UPDATE (Feb. 17) -qCFW and Future potential(Forum Thread)by@aomsin2526>here<UPDATE (Feb. 13) -BadWDSD/qCFW + HW Flasher/NoBT(Forum Thread)UPDATE (Feb. 11) -qCFW files have been temporary removed from github by the developer, due to a bug, see@aomsin2526's post >here< & >also here<UPDATE (Feb. 3)-PS3HEN v3.4.1 (STABLE) has now been releasedby@esc0rtd3w, graduating from the 3.4.1 BETA builds. (Note: v3.4.1+ is required for qCFW)​

mtrl.desc: {CFW Release} 4.92.2 Evilnat Custom Firmware (w/ Cobra v8.5) - Includes: CEX/PEX/D-PEX) + noBD/BT

br.div: After a successful Open Beta for 4.92.2, PlayStation 3 CFW developer@Evilnathas nowOFFICIALLYupdated hisEvilnat Custom Firmware'sfor the PS3 to version4.92.2,A number of new changes and options have been added in this release for your PS3 FAT and Early Slim models capable of installing a CFW PUP.Various patches to the CFW have been made as well as various new additions to theCobra Payload, Also theXAI Pluginwas not left out, as there are some new options one being a newNew Overclock menuhas been introduced to the firmware. Now we no longer need a dedicated PUP to change overclock settings, it can be done directly on the XMB. All the details and changes can be found in Evilnat's release note's found below and also in the link provided at the bottom.​

mtrl.desc: BadWDSD HW Exploit - With new powerful qCFW for PS3 Slim & SuperSlim NOR models on the horizon

br.div: Update (Jan. 30) -@esc0rtd3whas releaseda new beta, while not the FINAL (yet) it is the first beta HEN with the qCFW installer.Update (Jan. 29) -@aomsin2526has released thefirst public version of qCFWfor the BadWDSD exploit, now we still needPS3HEN 3.4.1 (FINAL)for the qCFW installer to utilize the qCFW release.Update (Jan. 15)-@aomsin2526has provided a new progress report,qCFWis nearing a public release.>>>LinkUpdate (Dec. 8) -@aomsin2526(aka chattrapat_s / kafuu)has provided an update for the project, in the form of a "To-Do" list for the project, with some reported progress like"qCFW is done"see full post>>> LinkUpdate (Sept. 11) -New video demonstration and details "qCFW will be based on Evilnat PEX from now on. This means both CEX and...​

mtrl.desc: PS3HEN w/ Linux & Overclocking Support? BadHTAB A hardware-software based hypervisor (lv1) exploit

br.div: UPDATE 4 - (May 2 / June 9):@esc0rtd3whas created a fork of the BadHTAB Exploit with some changes such as an "Automatic reboot if glitch crashes ps3" feature among other changes to make it easier for testing the new exploit.(June 9)- ReadMe Updated in link belowUPDATE 2 & 3:Now, not only Linux but also Overclocking on PS3HEN is now possible w/BadHTAB exploit&PS3HEN (beta builds)that are currently supporting the exploit. This is still considered an experimental exploit & features soUse at your Risk. (See update tabs/links) for additional details and information on these advancements for the PlayStation 3.Original Article: Could Linux Support onPS3HENbe a reality with new exploit? Yes, and also other features but itsnot going to be for everyone. There is a newProof of Conceptreleased for the PS3 that has been implemented byKafuu(@aomsin2526)usingGeohot'soldhtab glitchon PS3HEN. Developer@zecoxaofirst started posting about this idea and the concept in Jan. of 2023 here inthe forum'sand has shared various thoughts and progress, with the latest progress being Kafuu's implementation.BadHTABis ahardware-softwarebased hypervisor (lv1) exploit,The exploit has a very low success rate of (5-10%)as GameOS has a much smaller "glitch window". As the readme states this not meant "for daily driver, it is for user's withskill and patienceonly.".​

mtrl.desc: PlayStation Home Revived and Enhanced by Home Laboratory!

br.div: Home is back on the XMB in 2025! Download and Play just like it was originally!I am pleased to present our extensive work on reviving and enhancingPlayStation Home. Led by AgentDark447, our team has developed an advanced, open-source iteration of this server platform, significantly expanding its capabilities beyond the original. Below, I outline the project’s scope, technical advancements, and access instructions for those interested in exploring or contributing.​

mtrl.desc: PS3 Toolset (by bguerville) with 4.92 support has been released.

br.div: If you have a Custom Firmware compatible PlayStation 3 Model and are running 4.92 Official Firmware and need to Jailbreak for the first time or if you updated your previous CFW installation by mistake to 4.92 OFW. Then the PS3 Toolset will exploit your PS3 via the console's web browser and visitingps3toolset.com website(see tutorial tab), then once the process has completed you will be able to install a Custom Firmware (such as 4.92 Evilnat) once again or for the first time.Your PlayStation 3 model must be able to install a CFW, these would be all the FAT models PS3 and Early production Slim Models. The easiest and most effective ways to determine if your PS3 can install a cfw​

mtrl.desc: 4.92 Evilnat Custom Firmware (w/ Cobra v8.5) Released - CEX,PEX,D-PEX,DEX builds

br.div: UPDATExai_plugin:New Overclocking Tools (change on the fly) / New ps2netemu improvements added.Following the4.92 firmware updatedeveloper's from around the community have been updating various projects. This time around PS3 CFW developer@Evilnatdrops by the forumand leave's us with a suite of ps3 custom firmware's that support 4.92 firmware. 4 Flavor's (CEX, PEX, D-PEX & DEX) are available for 4.92 Evilnat CFW, depending on your console/taste, all builds are equipped with theCobra v8.5 payloadto provide expanded feature set over Standard CFW's.If you are already on PS3 CFW, updating is a breeze, you just need to install the new CFW PUP file via the official USB Method and you will be on Evilnat's latest release. If you have updated to 4.92 OFW you will have to wait, as you will have re-exploit your ps3 console to allow for a Custom Firmware installation once again. The good news is that developer@bguervilleshould be rolling out a new update soon for the PS3 Toolset that support 4.92 firmware. The offline tool CFW Flash Writer may take longer for the community to update it, but we hope to see an update for that tool soon as well.​

mtrl.desc: PS3HEN 3.4.0 + HFW 4.92 - Officially released by esc0rtd3w of Team PS3Xploit

br.div: PS3 Developer@esc0rtd3whas released the latest update toPS3HENwith support to recent firmware updateof 4.92. Along with the PS3HEN update is the release ofHybrid Firmware (HFW), which is just an official firmware (4.92) with the "silk_webkit.sprx" file replace from 4.82 OFW (that has a vulnerability patched in 4.83 OFW, the last attempt Sony made to curb homebrew). HFW was created by@Joonieand has been apart of this 4.92 release of well.There have been a number of new changes in this update marked v3.4.0 as it does not just provide 4.92 support but various changes as seen in the changelog provided​

mtrl.desc: PS3 4.92 Official FIrmware has been released - PS3 CFW & PS3HEN DO NOT UPDATE to 4.92

br.div: As expectedthe PlayStation 3 has receivedfirmware update 4.92, if you are currently usingPS3HENorCFWyouSHOULD NOT updateyour PS3 Firmware to 4.92 at this time. Otherwise you will be temporary losing your jailbreak's and ability to run homebrew on the console, until developer's release new exploits/jailbreaks that support the new 4.92 firmware update. There isa processin which need to take place for things to catch up, as developer's@bguerville@esc0rtd3w@Evilnatand other's havebeen readyfor the update to launch to get to work quickly.We learned last week that@bguervillehas an update for the PS3 Toolset with new features being deployed as the developer explains in these two post >1<​

mtrl.desc: Update 4.91.3 > > > [PS3 CFW] Evilnat's 4.91.2 Custom Firmware's Collection Released

br.div: UPDATEJanuary 2025:CFW Evilnat 4.91.3[BETA 1] released>>Click Here<<December '24:Merry Christmas and Happy Holiday's to everyone, PS3 Custom firmware developer@Evilnathas dropped by the psx-place forum's on this Christmas day to present the final build(4.91.2)of the developer's ps3 Custom Firmware suite, after a series of Open Beta releases, v4.91.2 now officially drops the "BETA" tag and in its final form for 4.91.2. For discussion and full details about this PS3 firmware release and the various cfw types offered in this suite of releases, checkout the developer's release thread here in the forum's detailing all the various features and functions in Evilnat's latest releases notes and details for4.91 Evilnat's CFW (w/ Cobra v8.5).​

mtrl.desc: PS4 EMC (SouthBridge) CFW for PHAT Models CUH-1000 / CUH-1100 (Tutorial by zecoxao)

br.div: Update (Additional Tutorial):Software method for 5.05 only(5.05 hackable CUH-1000 and CUH-1100 ONLY!):We had some interesting developments on thePlayStation 5's 4.03 firmware just recently, Now developer@zecoxaohas dropped news for the PS4 in the psx-place forums today with a new tutorial that i must say interesting for the PlayStation 4 (PS4) community, asPS4 PHAT Models (CUH-1000 / 1100)now has theability for a CFW (in the southbridge emc that acts like a second syscon). Now, this is an advanced task and you can do some damage to your PS4 if you do not follow the instructions and even so there is always a risk when your messing with internal flash (EMC in this case) ,So this is aUSE AT YOUR OWN RISKrelease.You have been warned of the potentials.This works on firmware upto 9.03, also works on devkits / early testkits From@zecoxao"This only grants permanence over the southbridge and syscon, but does not grant permanence over samu it's just over southbridge and syscon you can think of it as a second syscon" So in laymen terms this is not like your traditional PSP/PS3 CFW, but rather a firmware for the EMC chip not the actual PS4's firmware (don't confuse the two).,The CFW (EMC) will allow for various things like " making the console beep, change temperature limits, increase or decrease fan speed etc"​

mtrl.desc: Icon Mask ( update v1.05) Change the appearance of your PS4 icons

br.div: Update v1.05 ReleasedOriginal article: DeveloperLapyhas a new utility ps4 homebrew he has released recently along with two quick updates to provide additional functionality with the release ofIcon Mask. Many of you know Lapy from his various homebrew creations on the Ps4 / PS Vita. We have seen from Lapy Games titles likeDuck Hunt remake/Wild Gunman RemakeBrain Splitter& various others you can see in theLapy Games Collection. the developer has released various utility applications likePS4-Xplorer/PS4 Tempature&PS4 App LockNow withIcon Maskthe developer has gave user's an easy way to customizes Icons on the PS4's main menu, giving a custom look to the UI with a variety of "mask" that can be used with more planned to be added in the future.. Below are some of the details of Icon Mask (which currently sits at v1.02 and supports 9.00 FW)​

mtrl.desc: pFBNEO (FBA) / pSNES (SNES9x) / pNES (Nestopia) Released for PS4 (9.00 FW) ports by Cpasjuste

br.div: You may know developerCpasjustework from the PS Vita with releases likepFBA/pSNES / pNES, well the developer recently released 3 emulator ports for the PS4.pFBNEO(a Final Burn Alpha emulator)/pSNES(SNES9X (Super Nintendo) Emulator/pNES(Nestopia (Nintendo NES) emulator).These emulators are compatible with the 9.00 jailbreak. View each below for the corresponding emulator details​

mtrl.desc: [Update 3- v1.0.0 Released] Apollo Save Tool (new PS4 version) Released by bucanero

br.div: Update 3 (Jan. 30 2022)-v1.00 ReleasedFrequent PS3/PS4 developer seen in the psx-place forums@bucanerohas now officially released a new homebrew for the PS4(graduating from somerecent Beta testbuilds), Bringing a PS4 version of his popular PS3 homebrew utility for handling and managing saves games now for the PS4 Jailbroken user's(9.00 Support currently not supported, but will bein next update), TheApollo Save Toolis a homebrew app that runs on a homebrew enabled PS4 console and as PS3 CFW / HEN user;s will tell you its been a very useful utility for Save Games Management over the past couple of years with lots of handy features and frequent updates that have brought alot of new functionality and features. .Recently, the developer decided to try and bring this concept and homebrew tp the PS4 and the developer has managed to do just that with this recent release for the PS4. The development journey can be seen in hisdevelopment threadthat tracked progress of the PS4 version from the developerfound herein the forum's( also, here is the development thread forthe PS3 versionand also therelease threadhere for the PS3 app for those interested with a PS3.).View all the details of the latest PS4 release from developer@bucanerofound below​

mtrl.desc: [Update v1.2] SATURN-FPKG - Convert Saturn Games into PS4 fPKGs!

br.div: Following the recentPS4 9.00 Jailbreak, Ps4 developer@Jabuhas a new tool for 9.00 FW , you may remember this developer's previous release's for the PS4 with theps2-fpkgandpsx-fpkgtools that allowed for Ps2 / Ps1 games into a fpkg's to install on a homebrew enabled PS4. Following that same concept of injecting custom roms into an fpkg the developer now has a new tool that convertsSega Saturngames into PS4 fpkgs. With the release of "Cotton Guardian Force Saturn Tribute"(a Sega Saturn Game)for the PS4, it has equipped a useful Sega Saturn emulator that is said to be based off theSSF emulator. (According to ZiL0G80) and performing well. Upon seeing this game release is when developer@Jabuinterest peaked and then decided to develop and release the following tool to make running your Sega Saturn games easily on your homebrew equipped PS4 (9.00 FW).....​

mtrl.desc: PS4 Firmware 9.00 Jailbreak Update (Released)

br.div: "In this project you will find an implementation that tries to make use of a filesystem bug for the Playstation 4 on firmware 9.00. The bug was found while diffing the 9.00 and 9.03 kernels. It will require a drive with a modified exfat filesystem. Successfully triggering it will allow you to run arbitrary code as kernel, to allow jailbreaking and kernel-level modifications to the system. will launch the usual payload launcher (on port 9020).- via project's official readme"PS4 Firmware 9.00Jailbreak Released(awesome work bychendochap&@Znullptr)​

mtrl.desc: {UPDATE] PS4 Jailbreak 7.50 /7.55 w/ Mira Support Released

br.div: UPDATE7.55 w/ Mira Support ReleasedOriginal Article (7.50) Not only did we just see aPS1 exploit released, the exploited PS4 firmware latter has extended a step to now firmware 7.50 has been officially exploited and released to the public as developerSleirsgoevyhas released the latest PS4 Jailbreak that has utilized the latest findings fromTheFlowin his disclosed bug that has the potential to work upto 7.55 but is currently targeted for 7.50 for this release.TheFlowhas tweeted several hints regarding 7.55 (tweet1/2) but 7.55 is yet to be solved by other,@zecoxaoprovided some details and shared via twitter see below.This is not a fully functional jailbreak yet as there is elements that are needed to be ported like the Mira/HEN and other Payload, so you should not update to this exploit from previous exploits yet. Let it mature, but if your on a PS4 on 7.50 or lower and missed out on previous exploits, Stay Tuned as this progresses,​

mtrl.desc: PS4 Tools (Library w/ Unity Support) updated with "huge update" by The Darkprogrammer

br.div: Alongside of the recent release ofPS4 Trophy UnlockerPS4 developer@The Darkprogrameralso had a big update forPS4 Tools, a Open Source suite of tools in all-in-one library. Various projects have utilized this library in their projects likePS4's PS2 Classic GUI,PS4 PKG Tool, ,PS4 Xploreralong with various other tools (listed below). but the dev is back with new updates for developer;s who may want to use this library that supports Unity development. In this "huge update" from@The Darkprogramerdevs now have the ability to use the recently releasedTrophy Unlockerand other Recovery Tools in their projects. Not really an end-user release but useful for developer;s who especially have used Unity in creation of their projects. The DarkProgrammer has a homebrew he hopes to release very soon also utilizing this library. .​

mtrl.desc: PS4 Trophy Unlocker v1.0 by The Darkprogramer

br.div: PlayStation 4 (PS4) developer@The Darkprogramerhas released a new project for the community calledPS4 TrophyUnlocker. There is a PC utility that will unlock the trophies using a new method that relies on the game files itself to achieve, It will create a new pkg of the game installation, so if you have it installed already, be aware some files could potentially get lost if your have to overwrite your game. So be aware of that going in also this project isstill in the BETA stage, (v1.0 Released) so expect some bugs and different results depending on the game's trophies your unlocking. if you do experiance an error report it to the developer directly or in the comments below.. Also take note of the another recent release by this developer for developer with theupdate toPS4 Tools​

mtrl.desc: The Power Supply (Vol. 08) - Our Guest Today: "well-known multiconsole Developer @CelesteBlue123"

br.div: Since you liked ourpast Interviews so farwhere we talk withDevelopers across all PlayStation consoles- and especiallyone of our latest Interviewwhere wetalked with a Developer who tinkers with various consoles simultaneously- we thought about to do this again. Today's Guest is alsowell-known for releasing various Homebrew Applicationsmainly for thePlayStation Vita, but also for thePlayStation 4. In fact in this interview, you will learn not only what he released so far for both consoles, but alsowhat it means to develop for the PS Vita as for the PS4 in terms of "connectivity" for both consoles. But what does this mean exactly? Maybe you can remember back when the PS4 got presented back at E3 2013,Sony itself tried to make it more tasty for you not only to play with their newest Home Console, but also with the previous released PS Vita as well, especially when using the official "Remote Play" Feature. While this didn't worked quite well for Sony to be honest -although even the newest PS4 Games today still supports "Remote Play"- this Developer took a similar approachto understand the "connectivity" between both consoles but in terms of hacking them and to create useful Homebrew for it. And his results are impressive as well. So without further ado,our Guest Today is well-known multiconsole Developer@CelesteBlue.​

mtrl.desc: Movian M7 (by deank) - "libav" swapped for "ffmpeg" & slight name change

br.div: Developer@deankwho has been continuing development of the (modded version) of the original media center that started on the PS3(now runs on Android, Amazon Fire Stick, MacOS ,Windown ,Linux & Raspberry pi). The media center originally was named "Showtime" on the PS3 but later renamed to "Movian" by the original developerAndreas Omanyears ago.Since that time, we have not seen the official project do much in recent years (currentlyhome siteis down as of writing), but developer deank has been keeping the project alive and evolving (see changelogsover at brewologyfor all archived changes). One of the biggest changes you will see within Movian-M7 compared to older version, is the change from the older (abandoned) "libav" video decoders/parsers to the current "ffmpeg" that was just replaced in the recent M7 update . This should improve compatibility and make for a more up-to date and versatile media player for all supported platforms including the PS3.Also, deank has made a quick stop in the forum's, then to see a reported issue and provided a fix (seen here) that is now present in v7.0.2​

mtrl.desc: [Update v1.06] Itemzflow (PS5) - Homebrew & Game Manager with PS5 game Support

br.div: Following the release ofetaHEN(AIO Homebrew Enabler for the PS5)last month from@lightingModz.Now, the release ofItemzFlowhas arrived for the PlayStation 5 from developer @ligthingmodz. Originally thisHomebrew & Game Managerwas released on the PS4, but the developer has made a special Christmas Release now bringing the homebrew app for the PS5 that is handy for managing and preserving your personal backups.(JUN 27, 2024) Update: v1.06has been released with new improvements and features.​

mtrl.desc: Alien Resurrection - (A cheat code to unlock boot disc feature for backups)

br.div: This is a very interesting discovery or rather disclosure from one of the developer's of the PlayStation 1 (PS1) gameAlien Resurrection(Martin Piper) who had reached out to YouTuberMVGwho has been given permission to disclose something that has always been there all this time in the game, but needed a cheat code to unlock the ability to run backups on a stock retail PS1, acting as a boot disc for the swap method. MVG details the process here in this video:​

mtrl.desc: [Update v1.8B] etaHEN - AIO Homebrew enabler for the PS5

br.div: The PlayStation 5 has an early Christmas gift this year as developerLighting Modshas releasedetaHEN - AIO Homebrew Enabler for the PlayStation 5 (PS5), Currently there is not much homebrew to launch for the PS5, but the developer hasported his PS4 HB Store to the PlayStation 5and thanks to theability to run PS4 fpkgs on the PS5, there is various PS4 homebrew that can run such as some of Lapy's homebrew games.Update (Aug. 12):New update has been released by @ligthingmods, now bringing etaHEN to v1.8B, See changelog tab below or guthub links for latest changes and features incorporated in this update and other updates since its release that has jump started the PS5 community. .​

mtrl.desc: Celebrating Nine Year's of PSX-Place.com

br.div: The psx-place.com website celebrates its 9th birthday on this day, originally opening in2014onOctober 14to the public. The site remains a pillar in the PlayStation Homebrew / Hacking community, thanks to the tremendous developer & user support that has blessed our forums. The past 9 years we have witnessed some amazing things in these forums, we have seen idea's and theories mature into full blown exploits and projects, we have seen developer's share and bounce idea to each other and even teamed up, which have spawned and improved many projects/aspects in the community. We have seen many developer's choose the psx-place forum to host their Release/Support threads for their projects. It has been a destination for developer's and user's to learn more about the community and even take part in it. Truly a place where people can come together for a common goal and that goal has been getting the most out of our beloved PlayStation devices.​

mtrl.desc: Athena - A performant JavaScript Runtime Environment for the PS2

br.div: AthenaEnv (JavaScript-Runtime) and the bright future of development on the PS2!​

mtrl.desc: FuHEN Contest Submissions - The Four of Us Are Dying / Beat2x / Zik-Zak Vita / Sonic SMS 3 Timelines

br.div: The FuHEN - PS Vita Contest is underway and we have already 4 submissions that have been approved for entry: We have seen 3 Game Ports submitted and also a new Original Game for the PS Vita. If you have not heard yet, there is a a PS Vita Contest that is currently on going and the submission period for projects is fromJuly 22nd, 2023 September 22nd, 2023You can learn more about the contest prizes, rules and the submissions process at the official website.We have an ambitious Original Game that has been in the making for awhile and aims to provide AAA graphics and effects. The gameplay is still early but the developer has big plans for the game called "The Four of us are Dying".byJosh Allen / noradninja.this game is a Silent Hill 2/Alan Wake style survival horror game.In the Game Port category we have seen 3 entries approved we have seen PS Vita legendary developer@Rinnegatamanteprovide three projects the first being"Beat2k" (A Dance Dance Revolution Clone)and also the developer has entered"Zik-Zak Vita"which is a great game that testes your reaction time getting through each level , Then@Rinnegatamantealso submitted "Eldritch" is a first person shooter inspired by games like roguelikes, immersive sims, and H. P. Lovecraft. Then tou round out the first wave of entries we have seen developer@MDashKenter"Sonic SMS 3 Timelines"which is port of a fan made Sonic game that takes Sonic on a new adventure.​

mtrl.desc: The Future of the PlayStation 2 Internal Hard Drive by Berion

br.div: The Future of the PlayStation 2 Internal Hard DriveWe have recently started 2023, and the so-called PlayStation 2 console scene is surprisingly still doing well, yes, even better than ever before and better than on not one newer platform! And one of the leading projects that anyone interested can mention without a long moment's hesitation isOpen PS2 Loader- the successor to HDLoader and USB Advance, a program used to run games from USB, i.Link (FireWire), MX4SIO (a reader for e.g. SDXC in the Memory Card slot), the local network and the internal hard drive, whose operation is still stuck in the darkness of the Middle Ages.Before I get into specifics, it's worth recalling what the current, extremely exotic logical disk structure that Sony designed looks like, and which is supported by every software written to date that supports theinternalhard drive. Without understanding the APA, you won't understand the idea of a hybrid drive. It is only later that I will discuss the concept of building a Frankenstein with something modern, familiar, while providing a full or residual layer of compatibility.​

mtrl.desc: (Update) Baryon Sweeper: Unbrick PSP 1000, 2000, 3000 /PSP Slim 04g, 07g, 09g/ PSP Street 11g model

br.div: Update (May - 2023):Now PSP models Slim 04g, 07g, 09g models, & the PSP Street 11g model are now supported >> See Details from@zecoxaoin the post via>>this link<<Original Article (Jan 2021): For more than ten years PSP-3000 owner's have been used to the fact that their device couldn't be restored (unbricked) at home. Many fear the brick as if it's the worst nightmare that could ever happen when experimenting with your device. Even worse in 2021, now because warranty for all PSPs are over, so bricked later (psp) models go either for parts or are doomed to dust in boxes and drawers they'd never return from... that is, if this thread didn't appear with the release ofBaryon Sweeper R1:^)​

mtrl.desc: PS3HEN v3.2.2 - New Additions/ Fixes & Backported Firmware's Support from Team PS3Xploit.

br.div: Update 4/25/2023: PS3HEN 3.2.2 has been released. Check the changelog for details.We have seen a lot of developer's back in the fold after the4.90 official firmware updatethat hit the PlayStation 3 several weeks ago. We seen a couple weeks ago developer's@Joonieand@esc0rtd3wof thePs3Xploit Teamupdate Ps3HEN to v3.2.0, now we have a new update with the release of v3.2.1..Ps3HENis a Homebrew Enabler that is an alternative exploit to custom firmware (cfw) for models that can not install a CFW. unlikecustom firmwareon the ps3, Ps3HEN has the ability to run on all ps3 model's, while many feature that are in CFW can be found in PS3HEN it does not completely replicate the CFW experience so if your PS3 has the ability to run a CFW that is the better route, but Ps3HEN is a very powerful and capable exploit. See a CFW vs Ps3HEN comparison>here<for a rundown of feature's.In the update v3.2.1, there has been a number of changes and added support to Ps3HEN.@Jooniehas released a backport ofHFW (hybrid firmware) for 4.83, this was done as Ps3HEN has provided support for 4.83 FW, plus 4.84 DEX firmware is also now supported. There has been a number of other supported firmware and various other changes in the payload and plugin to PS3HEN, Plus this fixes the wMM bug that was creating freezing issue in v3.2.0. See latest changelogs provided below.​
